text:
Team GB medallist Lewis Richardson says it would be ‘extremely damaging’ and ‘an absolute travesty’ if boxing is removed from the Olympic programme for the Los Angeles Games in 2028.

Two sports will be dropped from theParisGames and six new sports are set to be added to the schedule in LA in four years’ time.

It’s been confirmed that breaking will scrapped after making its Olympic debut in the French capital to a mixed reception thissummer.

Meanwhile, squash, flag football, baseball, softball, lacrosse and cricket are in line to be added to the agenda in the States.

As it stands, boxing is at serious risk of being axed, despite the sport featuring in almost every Summer Games since its introduction way back in 1904 in Missouri. Since then, the only year it has not made an appearance was 1912, when laws in Sweden prohibited the sport.

The International Boxing Association (IBA) was stripped of its status as the global governing body for boxing by the Intentional Olympic Committee (IOC) in June 2023 after it failed to complete reforms on governance, finance and ethical issues.

Looking ahead to 2028, the IOC have warned that boxing has little chance of featuring unless it proves that it has cleaned up its act amid allegations of corruption, match-fixing and financial scandals.

Richardson, who claimed Team’s sole boxing medal in Paris with bronze in the men’s light middleweight category, hopes a resolution is found so that aspiring fighters are given the same platform that has benefitted him in his career.

‘To the athletes, to the boxers in general, I think it’s extremely damaging,’ Richardson told BBC Sport.

‘The Olympic Games just puts you on such a global platform. It’s the biggest stage in the world, it gives you a real opportunity to showcase your skills in front of everybody.’

Richardson added: ‘From an athlete perspective, it would be extremely damaging.

‘From a sporting, from an Olympic, from a UK Sport perspective, if boxing isn’t to be in LA I believe it would be an absolute travesty because boxing is such a special sport, it’s historical.

‘People love the gladiatorial aspect of boxing where men and women compete one-on-one to see who is victorious.

‘As I said, it would be a real shame so let’s hope World Boxing and the IOC can get together and make sure it does remain in LA because without boxing, the Olympic would certainly be missing a massive sport.’

Richardson is eternally ‘grateful’ for the ‘unlimited opportunities and experiences’ boxing has provided him in his career and is desperate for the sport to remain part of the Olympics in LA.

‘For me personally, boxing has changed my life for the massive, massive benefit. I’ve come from fairly humble beginnings and boxing has provided unlimited opportunities and experiences for me,’ he continued.

‘I’ve had some of the best moments of my life, including the last few weeks here at the Olympic Games, through boxing and that’s why I keep saying I’m so grateful.

‘I’m grateful for boxing, I’m grateful for the sport in general, and for me personally and for all the aspiring athletes, boxers coming through, let’s hope we can do whatever we can to ensure it does remain in LA.’

The IOC have encouraged national boxing federations to create a new global body or risk missing out on the Games in 2028.

At a press conference last week, IOC president Thomas Bach said a final decision on boxing’s inclusion would be made before the end of next year.

‘During the course of next year, as soon as possible,’ Bach told reporters.

‘But we cannot wait longer than the end of next year.’

title: Team GB medallist says it would be 'absolute travesty' if sport is scrapped for Olympics | Metro News
description: Team GB star Lewis Richardson says it would be a 'travesty' if boxing is removed from the Olympic programme for the LA Games in 2028.
